Course Title: AI for Staff Monitoring and Performance
Category: Hospitality & Tourism: Hotels, Tourism, Travel Agencies, Food & Beverage
Who is this for: Managers, supervisors, team leaders, department heads, hotel managers, restaurant managers, front desk supervisors, operations managers, human resources staff, hospitality managers
Team or Dept this is for: HR, Management, Operations, Hospitality Management, People Operations
Description: Privacy and trust concerns among employees.
Prerequisites: Learners should have a basic familiarity with everyday workplace technology (such as email, scheduling software, or point-of-sale systems) and a general awareness of how staff management operates within a hospitality or tourism setting.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key privacy concerns that arise when AI is used to monitor staff performance in hospitality and tourism settings, and explain how these concerns can affect employee trust and workplace culture. You will also apply practical strategies to communicate transparently with your team about AI monitoring practices, helping to build a fair and respectful environment for all staff members.
